items.title: China leaves benchmark lending rates unchanged as Beijing signals tolerance for stronger yuan
items.description: China's central bank kept its benchmark lending rates unchanged as Beijing manages a balancing act of supporting a slowing economy while maintaining currency stability.

(NASDAQ:SHOP) from Hold to Buy and raised his price target on the stock from $110 to $150. Tillman cited the main reason for the upgrade as an industry-wide drop in software stock […]

items.title: Financial Stocks Take a Hit With AI Worries on Investors’ Minds
items.description: Shares in big banks and other financial services firms took a hit on Monday with investors parsing through the possible impacts of artificial intelligence on the payment processing fees that bring in billions of dollars to the industry every year.  American Express’ stock was down over 7% as of Monday afternoon while JPMorgan, Citigroup and Morgan Stanley were all down 4% or more.  Investors were particularly focused on a report from Citrini Research that predicted an economic calamity based on the widespread proliferation of artificial intelligence into the economy.
